---
name: Autofix request
about: File a bug or small feature for Minesweeper to fix autonomously.
title: ""
labels: ["autofix"]
assignees: []
---

<!--
Filing this issue with the `autofix` label opts it into Minesweeper's queue.
Until the prompt-injection screen lands (plan 11), only file these issues
yourself — do not apply the `autofix` label to issues filed by external
contributors. See the "Bootstrap mode" section of the README for the full
rules.

Fill in every section below. The planner reads the body verbatim and bases
the execution plan on it, so the more concrete you are here, the closer
the resulting PR will be to what you actually want.
-->

## Problem

<!--
Describe the problem in one or two paragraphs. State observed behaviour
("clicking X produces Y") rather than guessing at the cause. If this is a
bug, include a minimal reproduction (commands, inputs, expected vs actual
output).
-->

## Acceptance criteria

<!--
A bulleted list of conditions that must hold for this issue to be
considered fixed. Each bullet should be objectively verifiable — a future
reviewer should be able to read the bullet and decide pass/fail without
asking follow-up questions.
-->

- [ ]
- [ ]

## Suggested approach (optional)

<!--
If you already have a sense of where the fix should go, jot it here. The
planner is free to ignore this and choose its own approach — the
acceptance criteria are what binds the implementation.
-->

## Out of scope

<!--
Anything you specifically *don't* want changed as part of this issue.
Helps prevent the executor from going on tangents.
-->

-

## References

<!--
Links to related issues, PRs, docs, or external resources. Optional.
-->

-
